text: The surgeon begins the parovarian cystectomy by incising the anti-mycentric border of the broad ligament. Using gentle traction and counter traction, the cyst is carefully separated from its bed within the mesentery of the broad ligament, taking care to avoid damage to the fragile underlying blood vessels.
The surgeon applies coagulation at the final point of attachment to facilitate the complete removal of the parovarian cyst.
The surgeon ruptures the parovarian cyst within the peritoneal cavity, allowing the fluid to escape. The cyst is then ribbonized, extracted, and prepared for histopathological examination.
